text:
Once upon a starry night sky. There grew an odd lilypad.

It bobbed.

It danced.

Then sang!

"Songbirds," quoth it!

That evening. Birdfolks saw.

They swooped.

They swooned.

They swooped!

And listened.

That was odd.

How would?

They hatted.

That little thing.

You couldn't hate lilypads!

So softly woozed they.

Soft whispers.

A gentle sway.

And sang —

"Lullabies."

Every evening.

That way.

The lilypad woozed.

And slept.

Dreamtime.
















































That's how birdlings knew.


Maybe.



That little critter.




Next morning.

Something else.




Just like us.







That’s okay.




All creatures.







Just find feather.




So owl.


































That whispers softly.




That's our world.


That whispers tales.




Just think.



That sounds fun.




With friends.




Sounds good.



I think.




That sounds great.



Well
